span.p: New signal proposed for the health tier

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Does the signal fire BEFORE the customer decides to churn - is it leading?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: EXCLUDE FROM TIER - use as post-mortem context only - lagging signals describe history not predict it

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Has it been back-tested against at least 30 completed renewals?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: ADD AS PROVISIONAL - label unvalidated-domain-default - schedule back-test for next renewal cycle

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Precision > 40% AND Recall > 30% at a usable threshold?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Does it add predictive power the current signal set lacks - no redundancy?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: ADD TO TIER RULE - document threshold and window - version the rule

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: ADD AS SUB-INDICATOR OR REPLACE - show in explainability panel or swap for the weaker existing signal

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: ADD AS SUB-INDICATOR ONLY - show in explainability panel - do not include in tier expression
